title: Rebuild Raj Empire Esports Arena — Full Platform

requirements.text: Build the complete backend actor with the following data models and logic: (1) Tournament model with fields: id, type (daily/mega), title, entryFee, prizePool, squadSize (Solo/Duo/Squad), upiId, qrCodeImage (blob), posterImage (blob, mega only), status (upcoming/ongoing/completed), slots, registeredCount, createdAt. (2) Player/User model with fields: id, displayName, mobileNumber, bgmiPlayerId, registeredAt. (3) Registration model with fields: id, tournamentId, playerId, paymentScreenshot (blob), status (pending/approved/rejected), registeredAt. (4) Support ticket model. (5) Social links model. (6) Terms & Conditions model. Expose CRUD functions for all models with admin-only write access.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin can create, update, and delete both Daily and Mega tournaments
Each tournament stores its own entryFee, prizePool, squadSize, upiId, and qrCodeImage independently
Mega tournament stores a poster image blob alongside entryFee and squadSize
Players can register for tournaments with a payment screenshot
Admin can approve or reject registrations
Social links, Terms & Conditions, and Support tickets are persisted and retrievable

requirements.text: Build a red-orange fire gradient themed frontend matching the reference screenshots: deep red (#C0100A) to bright orange (#FF6A00) gradient background on hero and key sections. Use Orbitron/Rajdhani fonts for headings. Apply the theme consistently across all pages — home, user panel, admin panel, login, registration.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero section shows red-to-orange diagonal gradient matching the screenshot
All headings use Orbitron or Rajdhani gaming fonts
Theme is consistent across all pages and components
Buttons, cards, and badges follow the red-orange brand palette

requirements.text: Build the public Home Page with: (1) Hero section with 'India's Premier BGMI Tournament Platform' badge, 'Raj Empire Esports Arena' heading, tagline 'Compete in thrilling BGMI tournaments, win real prizes, and prove you're the best player in the arena.', 'View Tournaments' and 'Join Now — Free' CTA buttons. (2) Feature highlights section. (3) Links to User Panel and Admin Panel. (4) Header with navigation and auth-aware actions. (5) Footer with social links (YouTube, Instagram, Telegram) fetched from backend.
acceptanceCriteria: Home page renders hero section exactly matching the reference screenshot
CTA buttons navigate to tournaments page and registration page respectively
Footer social links are fetched from backend
Header shows login/logout/dashboard based on auth state

requirements.text: Build the Player Registration and Login flow: (1) Multi-step registration collecting displayName, mobileNumber, and bgmiPlayerId, verified via OTP. (2) Two-step login with OTP verification. (3) Session persisted in localStorage via AuthContext. (4) ProtectedRoute redirects unauthenticated users to login.
acceptanceCriteria: Player can register with display name, mobile number, and BGMI Player ID
OTP is generated and verified through backend
Login persists session in localStorage
Protected routes redirect to login if not authenticated

requirements.text: Build the Player Dashboard with three tabs: (1) Tournaments tab — lists all tournaments with status-based filtering (Upcoming/Ongoing/Completed), shows entry fee, prize pool, squad size, available slots; player can click to register. (2) My Registrations tab — shows player's own registrations with status (pending/approved/rejected). (3) Profile tab — shows player's display name, mobile, BGMI ID.
acceptanceCriteria: Player dashboard shows three tabs: Tournaments, My Registrations, Profile
Tournament cards display entry fee, prize pool, squad size, and slot count
Player can initiate registration from a tournament card
My Registrations tab shows registration status for each entry

requirements.text: Build a Payment Modal for tournament registration that: shows the tournament's UPI ID and QR code image (fetched per tournament from backend), allows player to upload a payment screenshot, requires terms acceptance checkbox, and submits the registration. On success shows a confirmation state.
acceptanceCriteria: Payment modal shows the specific UPI ID and QR code for the selected tournament
Player can upload a payment screenshot
Terms acceptance checkbox must be checked before submission
Success state is shown after successful submission

requirements.text: Build the Admin Login page with username/password form (hardcoded credentials), show/hide password toggle, and redirect to admin dashboard on success. Admin session persisted in localStorage via AdminAuthContext.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin login page renders with username/password form
Show/hide password toggle works
Successful login redirects to admin dashboard
Failed login shows error message

requirements.text: Build the Admin Dashboard with a collapsible sidebar and the following tabbed sections: (1) Daily Tournaments — admin can create/edit/delete daily tournaments setting: title, entryFee, prizePool, squadSize (Solo/Duo/Squad), totalSlots, UPI ID, QR code image upload, status. Each daily tournament has its own independent UPI/QR. (2) Mega Tournaments — admin can create/edit/delete mega tournaments by uploading a poster image and setting: entryFee, squadSize (Solo/Duo/Squad), totalSlots, status. No extra text fields for poster. (3) Registrations — view all registrations, approve or reject with status update, view payment screenshots. (4) Players — view all registered players. (5) Terms & Conditions — edit and save T&C text. (6) Support Tickets — view and respond. (7) Social Links — set YouTube, Instagram, Telegram URLs.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin can create a Daily Tournament with independent entry fee, prize pool, squad size, UPI ID, and QR code per tournament
Admin can create a Mega Tournament by uploading only a poster image + setting entry fee and squad size
Mega Tournament creation form does NOT show extra text fields (no title/date/rules text inputs beyond entry fee and squad size)
Admin can approve or reject player registrations and view payment screenshots
Admin can edit Terms & Conditions text
Admin can set social media links
Admin dashboard is protected by AdminProtectedRoute

requirements.text: Build the Terms & Conditions page that fetches T&C content from the backend and renders numbered sections with colored headings. Include a hardcoded fallback if backend returns empty.
acceptanceCriteria: Terms page fetches content from backend
Numbered sections render with alternating colored headings
Hardcoded fallback content displays if backend returns empty

constraints: All Motoko logic must remain in a single backend/main.mo actor file
Do not edit any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Mega Tournament form must NOT include text fields for title, date, rules — only poster image upload, entry fee, and squad size
Each Daily Tournament must store its own UPI ID and QR code independently (not shared across tournaments)
nonGoals: Third-party payment gateway integration (UPI QR is display-only for manual payment)

required: Red to orange fire gradient hero background for BGMI esports platform, deep crimson red on the left transitioning to bright orange on the right (hero-bg.dim_1440x600.png)
